name: PC Management Best Practices: A Study of the Total Cost of Operation, Risk, Security, and Audit
description: IIA Research Foundation rough-draft report (Oct 7, 2003) authored by Mark Salamasick (University of Texas at Dallas) and Charles Le Grand (IIA AVP Technology Practices). Based on 221 GAIN flash-survey responses plus the July 2003 IIA Technology Audit Forum where Peter Kastner of Aberdeen Group was keynote speaker. Organized around the COSO Internal Control Integrated Framework and SAC model; covers business risk and insurance, Sarbanes-Oxley/HIPAA/GLBA compliance, information security and appropriate use, business continuity, software management, and PC TCO. Cites Kastner's research extensively on PC fleet TCO ($8k-$9k per seat), 20-25% cost reduction through best practices, 70-80% of TCO in operations, 40 million desktops running outdated OSes, and standardized images.
